news.headline: Verizon reports mixed Q3 results; sales at $33.33B miss estimates, EPS at $1.19 beats estimates; adds 239k postpaid phone customers, 11.9M broadband subscribers.
news.summary: Verizon Communications Inc (NYSE:VZ) released its fiscal third-quarter results which showed steady sales growth at $33.33 billion, slightly below the anticipated $33.43 billion. Despite a decline in wireless equipment revenue, service and other revenue growth compensated for it. The adjusted EPS stood at $1.19, surpassing the predicted $1.18. The period recorded 239,000 postpaid phone net additions and 349,000 retail postpaid net additions. A total of 389,000 broadband net additions were noted during the quarter. Verizon closed the quarter with 11.9 million broadband subscribers.

news.headline: Ferrari renews deal with Shell for multiple years starting 2026. Shell acquires 609MW power plant in Rhode Island.
news.summary: Ferrari N.V. (NYSE: RACE) has announced a renewed partnership with Shell PLC (NYSE: SHEL) that will extend for multiple years starting on January 1, 2026. The agreement will encompass support for Scuderia Ferrari HP, Ferrari Hypercar, and the Ferrari Challenge Series. In addition to this partnership, a subsidiary of Shell has finalized a deal to purchase a 100% equity stake in RISEC Holdings, LLC. RISEC Holdings owns a substantial 609-megawatt (MW) combined-cycle gas turbine power plant located in Rhode Island. The financial details of this acquisition have not been disclosed. This acquisition is significant as it ensures a long-term supply and capacity commitment for Shell in the deregulated market. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: 3M's Q3 earnings beat expectations with net sales of $6.068B and EPS $1.98 exceeding $1.90. FY24 EPS guidance revised to $7.20 - $7.30. Analysts bullish, raising price target to $160 from $143. View long road ahead with cyclical recovery and PFAS risks.
news.summary: Following impressive third-quarter results, analysts have increased the price target for 3M Company (NYSE:MMM). The company reported adjusted net sales of $6.068 billion, slightly surpassing the consensus of $6.057 billion, with adjusted EPS of $1.98 exceeding expectations of $1.90. 3M has adjusted its FY24 EPS guidance to $7.20 - $7.30 from $7.00 - $7.30 compared to the consensus of $7.26. BofA Securities analyst Andrew Obin raised the price target to $160 from $143, maintaining a Buy rating. He observed 3M's cautious outlook for 2025 due to limited macroeconomic visibility rather than a reflection of internal growth initiatives. The company has not identified a clear cycle inflection point, aligning with Obin's analysis.

news.headline: Wall Street down third day in a row; Dollar gains 0.5%, S&P 500 drops 0.7%, Dow Jones down 0.7%, tech stocks dip 0.9%.
news.summary: Wall Street is facing its third consecutive day of losses as investors await more clarity on U.S. fiscal and monetary policies, with equities and commodities declining while the U.S. dollar strengthened. The U.S. dollar index rose by 0.5% to levels last seen in late July, prompting investors to move to safer cash positions. The S&P 500 was down 0.7%, the Dow Jones dropped by 0.7%, and tech stocks and small caps slid by 0.9%. Mortgage applications declined for the fourth week due to high borrowing costs. Existing home sales fell by 1% in September to a rate of 3.84 million, the lowest since October 2010, missing forecasts of 3.9 million.

news.headline: LTK and Northwestern University release study on creator marketing for 2025, industry projected to reach $24B.

TFI International's Q3 results disappoint analysts, with earnings at $1.60/share and sales at $2.18 billion missing estimates. Dividend raised to 45 cents/share. CEO Bédard remains positive citing strong cash flow.
news.summary: TFI International (NYSE: TFII) announced disappointing Q3 earnings after Monday's market close. Their earnings stood at $1.60 per share, falling short of the anticipated $1.78 per share. The company also reported sales of $2.18 billion, missing the projected $2.25 billion. Despite this, TFI International raised its quarterly dividend by 13%, from 49 cents to 45 cents per share. Alain Bédard, the Chairman, President, and CEO, highlighted the company's robust performance, noting the generation of over $350 million in net cash from operating activities and more than $270 million in free cash flow, marking a 26% and 37% increase, respectively, from the prior year.

news.headline: Kroger and Walmart introduce digital tags amid speculation of 'surge pricing', but deny price hikes. Concerns from lawmakers unaddressed.
news.summary: Certain members of Congress have raised worries that retailers might begin surveilling customers and increasing prices. However, industry analysts have reported that the implementation of this technology has not led to a rise in grocery bills for customers.

Airband and Tarana partner to bring high-speed broadband to 265,000 UK locations using ngFWA technology.
news.summary: Airband, the UK's leading wireless internet service provider, has teamed up with Tarana, a provider of next-generation fixed wireless access broadband technology, to provide fast and dependable internet to 265,000 households and businesses in areas where fiber optic connections are unavailable. Through Tarana's innovative ngFWA platform, called "G1," users can enjoy high-speed internet comparable to fiber optics without the extensive time or expense of installation. This collaboration addresses longstanding challenges in the industry.

news.headline: QDOBA names Kevin Carroll as COO; achieves +7.7% comp sales in fiscal year to Sept 2024, following +6.3% growth last year.
news.summary: QDOBA has announced that Kevin Carroll will be heading restaurant operations as the company achieved a significant +7.7% increase in comparable sales for its fiscal year ending in September 2024, surpassing last year's growth of +6.3%. The company, based in San Diego, is recognized as the second-largest Mexican fast-casual restaurant brand in the United States.

news.headline: Starbucks' tough preannouncement includes a 3% decline in revenues, 7% drop in global sales, and 25% lower earnings. Bullish analysts support CEO Brian Niccol post-disappointing results.
news.summary: Market experts are assessing Starbucks Corp. (NASDAQ:SBUX) following its recent report of preliminary fourth-quarter results, falling short of expectations and a suspension of its 2025 guidance. The company revealed a projected 3% decline in consolidated net revenues, with a 7% decrease in global comparable sales. Earnings are forecasted at 80 cents per share, marking a 25% drop from the previous year. KeyBanc Capital Markets analyst Eric Gonzalez noted that while investors were bracing for disappointment, the extent of the revenue and profit miss may have caught them off guard. Gonzalez expressed faith in new CEO Brian Niccol, citing his successful leadership at Chipotle Mexican Grill. BofA Securities analyst Sara Senatore also highlighted Niccol's achievements at Chipotle.

Trader shares 3-wave strategy for meme coins like Dogecoin, Shiba Inu. Wave One is high risk-reward, Wave Two balanced.
news.summary: A crypto trader known as Hoeem has presented a trading strategy for navigating meme coin markets, which involves three distinct waves. Wave One is characterized by high risk and reward, a low win rate, and many rug pulls. Traders need advanced skills to identify vulnerabilities in smart contracts and assess wallet distributions, given the launch of numerous new coins daily. Wave Two presents a more balanced risk-reward ratio, a better win rate, and fewer rug pulls. Success here hinges on understanding social metrics and holder behavior. For more details on Hoeem's three-wave trading strategy in volatile meme coin markets, visit Benzinga.com.
